note: Fundamentals hand-verified from screener.in on 2026-07-05. Edit this file to update fundamentals (screener.in requires login for CSV export). Technicals are auto-computed by scripts/refresh.py via Yahoo Finance. The 'math' block per stock drives the Jul-Dec 2026 upside model: basis 'pe' means Price = EPS x P/E, basis 'pb' means Price = Book Value x P/B. anchor = today's EPS or book value per share; growth_pct = assumed earnings/book growth over the next ~2 quarters (deliberately BELOW recent actuals); target_multiple = the fair P/E or P/B if the market just returns to normal for this stock; div_6m_pct = roughly half the annual dividend yield.

stocks.name: Petronet LNG

math.multiple_note: traded 12-14x historically; 12.5x is below its own average
math.growth_note: Q4 profit already +25.6% YoY; we assume only +10%

stocks.thesis: PE 10.6 with ROCE 22.7% and near-zero debt, still 14% below its 52-week high. Handles ~75% of India's LNG imports; the post-ceasefire collapse in crude/LNG prices directly lifts regas volumes and spreads. Street targets: Motilal Oswal Rs 361, Investec Rs 400 (+31-45%). Cleanest fundamentals-plus-catalyst combination on the list.
stocks.risks: 3-yr sales CAGR is negative (LNG price passthrough optics); Dahej tariff renegotiation risk; volumes hostage to spot LNG prices.

confidence.why: Hardest catalyst on the list - spot LNG collapsed post-ceasefire; brokers already at 361-400

news.note: New Rs 1,200 cr long-term regas deal with Deepak Fertilisers arm at Dahej (starts May-Jul 2026); Q4 PAT beat estimates; MOFSL Buy TP Rs 361. Volume visibility improving - thesis strengthening.

holders.promoter: JV: GAIL + Indian Oil + BPCL + ONGC (12.5% each) = 50.00%
holders.trend: FIIs bought back in Mar qtr (26.3% → 27.1%); DIIs buying overall (10.9% → 12.9%)

stocks.name: Coal India

math.multiple_note: has traded 7-11x; 10x is the top of normal, not a stretch
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +13.7% YoY; we assume only +8%

stocks.thesis: PE 8.7, ROCE 35%, 6% dividend yield, D/E 0.12 - a cash machine that outperformed the falling market (+11.4% in H1 2026 vs NIFTY -7.1%) with price above a rising 200-DMA. Power demand plus 13.9% YoY GST growth says domestic volume is intact.
stocks.risks: Energy-transition derating cap on the multiple; government (63%) can demand special dividends or price restraint; e-auction premiums cyclical.

confidence.why: Smallest re-rating ask (8.7 to 10x), 6% cash-yield floor, trend already up while market fell
confidence.trigger: Q2 volumes (Nov) + any FPI return to cheap large-caps

news.note: Won Rs 2,831 cr solar LoA (Jalaun) + UP renewables JV; UBS Buy initiation. Watch: Q1 FY27 own output fell 7.5% even as June power-plant supplies rose 5.9%.

stocks.name: NMDC

math.multiple_note: mid-cycle for NMDC is 10-12x; 11x = middle of the range
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +37%, sales +34%; we assume less than half of that (+15%)

stocks.thesis: PE 10 with Q4 profit +37% YoY and sales +34%; 12-month return +30% with price 6% above the 200-DMA and 50>200 alignment - value with confirmed momentum. India's largest iron-ore producer riding domestic steel capex.
stocks.risks: Iron-ore price cyclicality; June metal-sector selloff (-6.5%) can extend; government divestment overhang.

confidence.why: Profit +37% with trend confirmed on all counts; iron-ore price is the swing factor

news.note: June production +44% YoY (5.15 MT); Q1 FY27 output +25.9% YoY. Volume momentum accelerating into FY27 - thesis strengthening.

holders.trend: FIIs buying 4 straight qtrs (12.2% → 13.6%); DIIs trimming
named: LIC 5.53% — largest public shareholder (long-term seller from 11.7% in 2023)

stocks.name: Karur Vysya Bank

math.multiple_note: quality private banks trade 12-16x; 12x is the cheap end
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +41% YoY with best-in-class NPAs; we assume +18%

stocks.thesis: Best asset quality in the screen (GNPA 0.75%, NNPA 0.19%) with Q4 profit +41% YoY at just 11x earnings. ROE 19.3%. Strongest technicals of all candidates: +7% above 200-DMA, 50>200, +30% over 12 months - and private banks led June's rally (+6.4%).
stocks.risks: No promoter (2.07%) - old-gen private bank governance discount; NIM compression if RBI resumes cutting; small-bank liquidity in a selloff.

confidence.why: Banks already leading (+6.4% June), cleanest loan book in the screen, modest 12x ask

news.note: Q1 FY27 business update: total business +15.9% YoY, advances +17.1%, deposits +14.9%; MD & CEO re-appointed 2 years. Clean hold; dividend record date Jul 24.

holders.promoter: Legacy founding families only 2.07% — institution-owned bank
holders.trend: FIIs buying strongly (+2.4pp in Mar qtr alone, 15.4% → 19.3% over 3 qtrs)
named: Rekha Jhunjhunwala 4.16% — held steady all 4 quarters (the only marquee individual in this entire screen)
ICICI Pru AMC cleared by RBI to go up to 9.95% (Feb 2026)

stocks.name: Life Insurance Corp

math.multiple_note: listed-life-insurer floor; private peers trade 2-6x higher

stocks.thesis: India's largest insurer at PE 9.5 with TTM profit up ~20%. The record Rs 32,000 cr/month SIP flow and DII dominance IS its business tailwind. Price 4% above the 200-DMA, mid-range in its 52-week band - unchased.
stocks.risks: Screener quarterly series is uneven (verify latest quarter before adding); market-share loss to private insurers; embedded-value opacity; government stake overhang.

confidence.why: SIP tailwind real, but the Rs 10,000 cr government stake-sale overhang + CFO exit likely cap the price near-term
confidence.trigger: Monthly SIP prints, Q2 embedded-value note

news.note: CFO Sunil Agrawal resigned effective Jul 14. Government (96.5%) preparing a fresh ~Rs 10,000 cr stake sale 'very soon' - OFS supply overhang likely caps the price inside the 6-month window. Confidence cut 45 to 40.

holders.promoter: President of India / Govt of India 96.50% (free float only ~3.5%)
holders.trend: FIIs slowly buying the tiny float; DIIs selling

stocks.name: Chambal Fertilisers

math.multiple_note: agri-input peers sit 11-14x; 11x is the bottom of that band
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +64% YoY; we assume just +12%

stocks.thesis: PE under 10 with ROCE 25.5%, near-zero debt, promoter 61%, and latest-quarter profit +64%. Price above a rising 200-DMA with 50>200. Agri-input demand is policy-protected; urea subsidy flow supports working capital.
stocks.risks: Subsidy-receivable timing swings quarters; gas price input risk; single-commodity (urea) concentration.

confidence.why: Policy-backed demand and clean books, but no dated catalyst - relies on gradual re-rate

news.note: Fertiliser stocks rallied up to 5% on sector tailwinds; Q3 revenue +20% YoY, virtually debt-free. No adverse news found.

holders.promoter: Adventz Group / KK Birla group (Saroj Poddar) 61.25% — and CREEPING UP (60.5% → 61.25%)
holders.trend: Promoters buying their own stock; FIIs trimming from Sep peak; DIIs buying Mar qtr

stocks.name: Infosys

math.multiple_note: 10-yr range 22-25x; 17x assumes it NEVER fully recovers
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +23% YoY; we assume +8% (guidance-cut buffer)

stocks.thesis: CONTRARIAN ENTRY - the deepest dislocation in NIFTY 500: -34% in H1 2026 on AI-disruption panic while earnings still grew (Q4 PAT +23% YoY). PE 14 vs 22-25 historical, ROCE 40%, dividend 4.6%. Kotak Buy FV Rs 1,220, PL Buy TP Rs 1,570 (+17% to +50%). Stagger the entry - it is 23% BELOW its 200-DMA (fails the trend gate).
stocks.risks: Falling knife until trend repairs; FY27 guidance cut risk flagged by Motilal Oswal; AI really is compressing legacy-services pricing; promoter holding low (founder-professional structure - normal for Infosys).

confidence.why: Biggest math upside but trend is broken - needs one non-disaster quarter to flip sentiment
confidence.trigger: Oct Q2 + FY27 guidance NOT being cut

news.note: Motilal Oswal: Infosys may CUT FY27 guidance this earnings season; ICICI Sec cut IT targets up to 33% ahead of Q1 (Jul 2). The July results are the event - this is exactly why the plan is 3 tranches, not a lump sum.

holders.promoter: Founders & families (Murthy, Nilekani, Gopalakrishnan, Shibulal et al.) 14.38%
holders.trend: Classic rotation: FIIs selling steadily (31.9% → 28.5%), DIIs absorbing (39.4% → 43.2%)

stocks.name: Hindustan Petroleum

math.multiple_note: OMCs trade 6-8x mid-cycle; 6x is the floor of the band
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +124% YoY; we assume ZERO growth (windfall normalizes)

stocks.thesis: CONTRARIAN ENTRY - PE 4.7 with Q4 profit +124% YoY and 6.1% dividend yield, 20% below its 52-week high after the H1 oil shock crushed OMCs. Brent collapsing post-ceasefire is a direct marketing-margin explosion. Just 5% below the 200-DMA - closest contrarian to trend repair.
stocks.risks: Government fuel-price interference in either direction; refining GRM cyclicality; D/E 0.85 is the highest among picks; crude re-spike if ceasefire breaks.

confidence.why: Crude collapse is physics for pump margins, but government pricing can take the windfall

news.note: Emkay Buy (12-29% OMC upside), MOFSL preferred OMC pick; Rs 7,920 cr LPG compensation flowing monthly through 2026. GRM + compensation tailwind intact.

holders.trend: FIIs buying 3 straight qtrs (13.6% Jun → 17.3% Mar, +2.8pp); DIIs selling

stocks.name: Bank of Baroda

math.multiple_note: banks are 'worth' ~1x book; BOB sells at 0.79x. 0.9x is still a discount
math.growth_note: ROE 12.7% adds ~6% to book value in 6 months

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - P/B 0.79 near its 52-week low while the Bank index rallied +6.4% in June; the un-chased laggard in the winning sector. 25-30% upside to mid-cycle P/B ~1.0. Enters the buy list when price reclaims the 200-DMA (currently 7% below).
stocks.risks: NMC Health settlement ~$600M dents Q1/Q2 FY27 earnings (Jul 2026); PSU credit-cost cycle; trend gate failed. Underlying growth strong (Q1 advances +17.4%).

confidence.why: 0.79x book is cheap but momentum still negative - entry gate is the 200-DMA reclaim

news.note: Definitive ~$600M (Rs 5,700-6,000 cr) NMC Health settlement - largest cross-border payout by an Indian PSB; shares slid ~7% in 2 days (this IS the capitulation volume on the chart). Underlying Q1 growth strong: advances +17.4%, deposits +13.8%. Verify the P&L hit in Q1 results before entering.

holders.trend: FIIs buying over FY26 (8.1% → 9.7%); DIIs flat

stocks.name: ONGC

math.multiple_note: sells below liquidation value (0.80x book); 0.95x is still sub-book
math.growth_note: retained profit adds ~3% to book in 6 months even in a weak year

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - P/B 0.80 near the 52-week low with a 5.15% dividend. Emkay 'Add' with 12-29% upside. Natural hedge against HINDPETRO (falling crude hurts ONGC, helps OMCs) if you hold both.
stocks.risks: Latest quarter profit -22% YoY (falling crude realization); ROCE 14.2% just under the 15% quality bar; windfall-tax regime risk.

confidence.why: Falling crude is a headwind for the seller of crude; 0.8x book + 5% div cap the downside more than they add upside

news.note: Emkay Add. KG-98/2 crude underdelivering (~28k bpd vs 45k target); peak gas expected mid-2026 with BP as technical expert; Rs 4,606 cr for 10 new KG wells. Execution drag priced in.

holders.promoter: President of India 58.89% (+ ~10.3% other govt-company cross-holdings)
holders.trend: FIIs mild buying; DIIs mild selling

stocks.name: HCL Technologies

math.growth_note: Q4 profit +4.2% YoY - slowest of all picks; we assume +4%

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - second IT contrarian: div 4.7%, near-debt-free, -28% in H1. INFY is the cleaner bet (cheaper PE, faster growth); hold this only if you want a second IT name. Market cap approximate. The math honestly shows ~15%, not 20%+ - that is why it is watchlist.
stocks.risks: JPMorgan underweight; PL Reduce TP Rs 1,300; 19% below 200-DMA; slowest profit growth among picks.

confidence.why: The honest math reaches only about +13% - tracked for information, not conviction

news.note: Same sector event as Infosys: possible FY27 guidance cut flagged by Motilal Oswal; ICICI Sec target cuts across IT. July results are the risk gate.

holders.promoter: Shiv Nadar family 60.86% (Vama Sundari Investments 44.21%; control gifted to Roshni Nadar Malhotra 2025)
holders.trend: FIIs selling 3 straight qtrs (18.6% → 15.5%); DIIs buying

stocks.name: Power Finance Corp

math.multiple_note: lenders re-rate on book value; PFC has ranged 0.8-1.3x - 1.2x is inside its own band
math.growth_note: ROE 20.7% compounds book ~9% in six months even with dividends out

stocks.thesis: India's biggest power-sector lender at 5.4x earnings and 1.06x book with a 4.4% dividend. Net NPA just 0.13% - the cleanest loan book of any PSU-linked lender. Every renewable-energy and grid-capex announcement flows through PFC/REC loan growth. Found by the full 500-stock systematic screen, then hand-verified.
stocks.risks: Wholesale power exposure concentration; state discom repayment cycles; rate-sensitive borrowing costs; government majority owner.

confidence.why: Cleanest NPAs in PSU-lender space, trend up, and the REC merger (approved Jul 1) is a re-rating catalyst - with integration volatility
confidence.trigger: Merger integration steps + Q2 NPA print; dividend record date Jul 31

news.note: REC merger into PFC approved by both boards (88 PFC shares per 100 REC) with presidential assent - creates a Rs 11 lakh cr power-financing giant. Re-rating catalyst, but expect swap-arbitrage volatility and integration noise. Rs 3.95 final dividend, record date Jul 31.

stocks.name: National Aluminium (NALCO)

math.multiple_note: metal producers run 10-14x mid-cycle; 12.5x is the middle
math.growth_note: Q4 +64% but company guides alumina SOFT at $300-310/t (Jun 2026) - we cut growth to +5%

stocks.thesis: World's lowest-cost bauxite/alumina producer at 11x earnings with ROCE 39.6%, essentially ZERO debt (borrowings Rs 182 cr vs Rs 63,841 cr market cap) and Q4 profit +64%. Price 22% below its 52-week high after the June metals selloff, yet still above a rising 200-DMA. Found by the systematic screen.
stocks.risks: Aluminium is a global cyclical - LME price and China supply decide the multiple; June metal weakness can extend; PSU divestment overhang.

confidence.why: Spectacular quality-plus-cheapness combo, but the commodity price is outside anyone's control
confidence.trigger: LME aluminium holding firm + Nov Q2 results

news.note: UBS initiated Buy on NALCO; CPSE metals rallied up to 7%. New 1-MTPA alumina refinery ~85% complete, commissioning from June. Caution: company itself guides alumina soft at $300-310/t on oversupply - growth assumption cut to +5% accordingly.

holders.trend: FIIs buying HARD 2 straight qtrs (16.2% Sep → 22.3% Mar, +6pp); DIIs trimming

stocks.name: Great Eastern Shipping

math.multiple_note: shipping mid-cycle runs 8-10x; 8.5x assumed with ZERO earnings growth
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +25.5% YoY but freight rates are volatile - we assume flat

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - India's largest shipper at 7x earnings, near-zero debt (D/E 0.065), Q4 profit +25%. Already +31% in 6 months with strong momentum. The catch: tanker/dry-bulk rates ARE the business, and nobody controls those.
stocks.risks: Pure freight-rate cyclical; promoter holding only 30%; already ran hard - 20% below 52-week high but well off lows; rate reversal erases the story fast.

confidence.why: Great math and momentum but the earnings driver (freight rates) is uncontrollable and mean-reverting

news.note: Buying a secondhand LR2 tanker; crude-tanker rates firm on OPEC+ unwind; analyst TP ~Rs 1,700. Cyclical rate reversal remains the core risk.

holders.trend: FIIs buying aggressively 3 qtrs (24.6% → 28.4%, +3.8pp); public shrinking

stocks.name: Tanla Platforms

math.multiple_note: traded 20x+ in its growth years; 16x assumes only a partial rehab
math.growth_note: FY26 full-year PAT nearly flat (+0.4%) despite Q4 growth - we assume only +5%

stocks.thesis: The only stock in the hidden-gems sweep that passed EVERY filter with zero screener flags: PE 14, ROCE 26.3%, essentially no debt, dividend-paying, promoter steady at 46%, sales rising every quarter of FY26 - yet 30% below its high purely because the tech rout tarred it. India's dominant CPaaS/messaging platform.
stocks.risks: CPaaS price commoditisation; tech-sector sentiment still broken after H1; mid-cap liquidity in a selloff.

confidence.why: Cleanest quality-per-rupee in the sweep, but FY26 PAT was flat - needs margin repair, not just sentiment
confidence.trigger: Q2 continuing the FY26 sales streak (Oct-Nov)

news.note: FY26 full-year: revenue +9.7% but PAT nearly FLAT (+0.4%) - margin compression despite the good Q4. Rs 175 cr buyback at Rs 875 completed (support done). Growth assumption cut to +5%; dead-money risk more than blow-up risk.

holders.promoter: Founder D. Uday Kumar Reddy 21.72% + group = 46.17%
holders.trend: FIIs and DIIs both nearly absent/selling — retail holds 45%; institutions have not discovered/returned yet
named: No marquee holders — no verified superinvestor positions

stocks.name: Gujarat Pipavav Port

math.multiple_note: port/infra peers run 17-22x; GPPL itself has averaged ~18x

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST (demoted Jul 5 on concession-risk news) - a debt-free port with ROCE 28%, profit +27%, an 83% payout funding a 5.3% dividend yield, APM Terminals (Maersk) as promoter - sitting near its 52-week LOW. The most unchased cash machine in the NIFTY 500. The dividend pays you to wait for the re-rate.
stocks.risks: MATERIAL (Jul 2026): concession expires Sep 2028, extension not secured, GMB MoU non-binding; CCO resigned Jun 30; single-port concentration; below 200-DMA.

confidence.why: 5.3% yield floor intact, but the 2028 concession extension is unresolved and binary - wait for clarity

news.note: MATERIAL: port concession expires Sep 29, 2028 and extension NOT yet secured (Rs 17,000 cr GMB MoU is explicitly non-binding); CCO resigned effective Jun 30. Binary concession news could land inside the window - demoted from pick to WATCH until extension clarity.

holders.promoter: APM Terminals (A.P. Moller-Maersk) 44.01% — operating rights until Sep 2028
holders.trend: FIIs buying 3 straight qtrs (19.5% → 22.1%); DIIs selling

stocks.name: Chennai Petroleum (CPCL)

math.multiple_note: refiners get 6-8x mid-cycle; 7x on NORMALIZED (lower) earnings
math.growth_note: Q4 profit +565% YoY is peak-cycle refining margin - we assume earnings FALL 15%

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - statistically the cheapest stock in the sweep (PE 5.4, ROCE 35%, profit up 6x YoY, IOC owns 67%). Kept off the buy list because the honest math says the PE is optically flattered: normalize the peak refining margins down 15% and the upside shrinks to ~10%. A trade on GRMs staying high, not an investment.
stocks.risks: Pure refining-margin cyclical - the E in the PE is peak-cycle; GRM reversal crushes it; thin dividend.

confidence.why: Cheap on peak earnings is not cheap - the math needs margins to STAY at cycle highs
confidence.trigger: Singapore GRM prints staying elevated through H2

news.note: Granted NAVRATNA status - greater capex/JV autonomy. Q1 results Jul 24. GRM volatility remains the story.

holders.promoter: Indian Oil Corp 51.89% + Naftiran Intertrade (NIOC affiliate, Iran) 15.40% = 67.29%
holders.trend: FIIs bought big Dec qtr (8.8% → 12.9%) and held; DIIs small buy

stocks.name: LIC Housing Finance

math.multiple_note: has ranged 0.9-1.4x book across cycles; 0.85x is still below the range
math.growth_note: ROE 14.4% compounds book ~6% in six months

stocks.thesis: WATCHLIST - the deepest sane value among financials: 0.74x book and 5.4x earnings for an LIC-parented mortgage book still growing (+8.7%). Just misses the buy list because ROE 14.4% sits under the 15% quality bar and rate cuts squeeze NIMs before they help volumes.
stocks.risks: NIM compression in the rate-cut cycle; asset-quality in developer book; ROE below the quality bar.

confidence.why: 0.74x book with LIC parentage caps downside, but the ROE does not force a re-rate quickly

news.note: Q4 PAT Rs 1,497 cr (+9% PBT), Rs 10/share dividend; next results Jul 30. Steady and cheap, no red flags.

holders.trend: FIIs buying Mar qtr; DIIs selling

stocks.name: Hero MotoCorp

math.multiple_note: traded 17-22x across cycles; 19x is mid-range for a franchise this profitable

stocks.thesis: The GARP find: world's largest two-wheeler maker at PE 16.6 with ROCE 35.8%, near-zero debt, a 3.8% dividend (71.5% payout) and TTM profit +37% - priced like a PSU while being a consumer franchise. 24% below its 52-week high. Adds the consumer-demand engine this PSU/commodity-heavy portfolio lacked.
stocks.risks: Two-wheeler volume cycle (5-yr sales CAGR only 8.9%); EV transition execution vs Ola/TVS; rural demand dependence.

confidence.why: Franchise quality at value price with a dividend floor, but volume growth is cyclical and EV share shift is real
confidence.trigger: Monthly volume prints + festive season demand (Oct-Nov)

news.note: June domestic dispatches -4.2% YoY (commuter fatigue) BUT exports +32.8%, VIDA EV registrations +185% YoY (11% EV share, brand refreshed), Rs 3,200 cr Andhra investment. Festive season (Oct-Nov) is the demand test.

holders.trend: FIIs buying strongly (27.1% Jun → 31.2% Mar, +4.1pp); DIIs selling

stocks.name: eClerx Services

math.multiple_note: quality midcap IT/KPO runs 20-25x; 21x is the low end

stocks.thesis: Niche high-margin KPO compounder (ROCE 34.8%, profit +34% YoY) derated 40% alongside large-cap IT despite growing twice as fast. Promoter 54.5%, returns cash via buybacks. Diversifies the IT exposure beyond INFY/HCLTECH into a segment AI augments rather than replaces (so far).
stocks.risks: Rides the same IT-services sentiment as INFY in the short run; client concentration typical of KPO; buybacks not dividends (no yield floor).

confidence.why: Growth is verified and the derating is sentiment-driven, but the sector tape must stop falling first
confidence.trigger: Q2 results + IT sector sentiment turning after July guidance season

news.note: FY26 profit +30.5% on revenue +22.3%; Rs 300 cr buyback completed Jan 2026 (2.1x oversubscribed); 1:1 bonus issued Mar 2026; promoters formally declared ZERO share encumbrance for FY26.

holders.promoter: Co-founders PD Mundhra + Anjan Malik ≈ 54.53% (rising via buybacks)
named: HDFC MF ~9.79% (HDFC Large & Mid Cap Fund 9.73% — largest public holder)

stocks.name: Larsen & Toubro
stocks.thesis: The single broadest expression of India's capex cycle — defence, metros, rail, power, water, hydrocarbons, semiconductors, heavy engineering. Order book crossed ₹5.7 lakh cr with 15-20% inflow guidance. Q4 profit +37%. Not cheap at 33x but the most diversified capex proxy on the exchange.
stocks.risks: Execution/working-capital on a giant order book; L&T Finance drag; 5-yr CAGR optically low (COVID base).

stocks.name: Power Grid Corporation
stocks.thesis: The cheapest way to own the grid supercycle: 16.7x PE, 3.15% dividend, ROE 16.5%, Q4 profit +15%. NEP targets ₹7.9 lakh cr transmission capex to evacuate 500GW renewables by 2030, plus AI data-centre load rising to 8-10GW. The transmission backbone every other government ambition runs through.
stocks.risks: Regulated returns cap upside; TBCB competition; high (but normal-for-utility) leverage.

stocks.name: CG Power & Industrial
stocks.thesis: Elite operator (ROCE 26.7%, near debt-free, Q4 +32.5%) in grid/motors/rail/semiconductor packaging — but priced at an extraordinary 118x PE, 17.9x book. Wonderful business, treacherous price.
stocks.risks: 118x PE / 17.9x book leave zero margin of safety; rising working-capital days flagged.

stocks.name: Polycab India
stocks.thesis: Cable & wire leader riding electrification — best ROCE in the group (33.2%). But 55.7x PE / 11.7x book with only ~7% latest-quarter profit growth is a demanding price.
stocks.risks: Multiple far ahead of current growth; copper-price and competition risk.

stocks.name: Siemens Ltd
stocks.thesis: Automation/rail/electrification blue-chip. Note: the -36% latest-quarter print is distorted by the Siemens Energy India demerger, not a real collapse. Quality is high; valuation at 52x is full.
stocks.risks: Demerger distorts comparability; 52x PE with no dividend post-reset.

stocks.name: KEI Industries

holders.trend: FII AND DII both buying (rare double accumulation)

stocks.thesis: Cables/electrification with the strongest institutional flow in the group — both FII (25.5→27.3) and DII (23.5→25.8) accumulating. ROCE 20%, low debt. The catch is a 53x multiple.
stocks.risks: 53x PE demands sustained 20%+ growth; commodity input swings.

stocks.name: Kalpataru Projects Intl
stocks.thesis: T&D + infrastructure EPC at a reasonable 23x with Q4 profit ~+83% and heavy DII accumulation (45% DII owned). One of the few electrification plays still at a sane multiple.
stocks.risks: EPC working-capital cycles; moderate leverage; order-timing lumpiness.

stocks.name: KEC International
stocks.thesis: The cheapest electrification EPC (20x, near 52-week low, 1.1% dividend) with a large T&D/rail/civil order book and a recovery in margins. DII stepping in as FII exits. Value entry in a high-policy theme.
stocks.risks: High working-capital debt; lowest ROE in the T&D cohort (11.3%); FII outflow.

stocks.name: Bharat Electronics
stocks.thesis: Best growth-adjusted defence name: ROCE 36.5%, Q4 profit +41%, near debt-free, and FIIs accumulating. Record ₹7.85 lakh cr defence budget (+15%) with 75% ring-fenced for domestic buy flows straight to its order book. 49x is rich but the least stretched pure-defence play on quality.
stocks.risks: 49x PE / 12.5x book; working-capital wrinkle (debtor days ~170) worth watching.

stocks.name: Hindustan Aeronautics
stocks.thesis: Cheapest defence PSU (33x) with the highest dividend and enormous order visibility (~₹1.9 lakh cr book). Post-Sindoor emergency procurement adds fuel. The value entry point in defence.
stocks.risks: Latest-quarter growth only +5.5% (execution pace); FII exiting; delivery timelines.

stocks.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders
stocks.thesis: Strongest fundamentals in shipbuilding: ROCE 43% (highest), ROE 31.8%, Q4 profit +42%, 36% 5-yr CAGR, near debt-free. Navy adds a warship every ~40 days from 2026. Premium price but earned.
stocks.risks: 40x PE; order-book lumpiness; low institutional float.

stocks.name: Data Patterns
stocks.thesis: High-quality private defence-electronics with 35% 5-yr CAGR and a clean balance sheet — but ~97x PE prices in flawless execution for years.
stocks.risks: 97x PE leaves no room for a single miss; customer concentration.

stocks.name: Solar Industries
stocks.thesis: Genuine compounder — highest 5-yr CAGR in defence (43%), ROCE 37% — expanding from explosives into ammunition/defence. But 98x PE and 26x book are the richest multiples in the set.
stocks.risks: 26x book / 98x PE = near-zero margin of safety; any growth stumble hurts hard.

stocks.name: Kaynes Technology

holders.trend: FII AND DII dumping hard (heavy institutional exit)

stocks.thesis: Deeper-in-the-chain electronics/OSAT play on India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, now 57% off its high. But single-digit ROE (9.6%) at 61x PE, and institutions are exiting aggressively — the flow is a red flag.
stocks.risks: Heavy FII+DII selling; 61x PE on 9.6% ROE; semiconductor theme carries an active China rare-earth headwind.

stocks.name: Dixon Technologies
stocks.thesis: The quality leader in electronics contract-manufacturing (ROCE 42%, ROE 37%) — the only name where returns nearly justify the multiple. But Q4 profit fell ~36% and 57x PE assumes the growth stall is temporary.
stocks.risks: Latest-quarter profit -36% (growth stalling); 57x PE; low promoter holding.

stocks.name: Cyient DLM
stocks.thesis: Electronics manufacturing for aerospace/defence/medical on the reshoring theme — but 57x PE against 7.5% ROE and a -29% latest quarter is a poor quality/price match.
stocks.risks: 57x PE on single-digit ROE; falling profits; near-zero FII interest.

stocks.name: Transformers & Rectifiers
stocks.thesis: The best value/quality in transformers (39x — cheapest, ROCE 23%, ROE 19%) — a direct grid-capex beneficiary as transformer demand surges. Cheapest of the equipment names, though DII is trimming and the latest quarter was flat.
stocks.risks: Still 39x PE; DII selling; order execution and copper/CRGO input costs.

stocks.name: Thermax
stocks.thesis: Energy/environment capital goods with a nuclear/clean-energy optionality — Q4 +18%. But 101x PE against a 10.6% ROE is one of the most stretched valuations in the whole universe.
stocks.risks: 101x PE on 10.6% ROE; order-cycle dependent; FII exiting.

stocks.name: VA Tech Wabag
stocks.thesis: Pure-play water treatment on the Jal Jeevan (extended to 2028) + ₹1 lakh cr Urban Challenge Fund theme. ROCE 21%, near-zero debt, Q4 +28%. Municipal water is a decades-long annuity. Trading near its 52-week high though.
stocks.risks: 36.8x near the highs; low promoter holding (19%); project-execution and receivables.

stocks.name: Hindustan Copper
stocks.thesis: India's only integrated copper miner — leveraged to the critical-minerals push and copper demand from electrification/EVs. ROCE 42%. But 48.7x PE and 14.4x book are extreme for a commodity producer, and the latest quarter was flat.
stocks.risks: 14.4x book / 48.7x PE on a cyclical commodity; flat earnings vs history; both FII+DII selling.

stocks.name: Biocon

holders.trend: FII AND DII both rising (accumulating despite weak fundamentals)

stocks.thesis: Biosimilars leader positioned for the BIOSECURE China-decoupling wave (₹10,000 cr Biopharma SHAKTI). Institutions are accumulating on the forward story — but trailing fundamentals are poor: 172x PE, 1.4% ROE, negative 5-yr CAGR, high debt, and a promoter holding that has fallen 16 points since 2024.
stocks.risks: 172x PE on depressed/negative earnings; ₹15,400 cr debt; promoter selling; turnaround is a bet, not a fact.

stocks.name: Syngene International
stocks.thesis: Leading CRO/CDMO — the cleanest BIOSECURE 'China+1' beneficiary as US pharma decouples from WuXi. Near its 52-week low. But 45.6x PE with a -32% latest quarter and flat 5-yr earnings needs the reshoring order flow to actually convert.
stocks.risks: 45.6x on declining profit; poor recent sales growth; BIOSECURE order flow is multi-year, not imminent.

stocks.name: Bharat Heavy Electricals

holders.trend: FII AND DII both rising (accumulating the turnaround)

stocks.thesis: The classic turnaround: Q4 profit +156% as thermal + nuclear (SHANTI Act, ₹24,000 cr Titagarh trainset-adjacent orders, SMR mission) orders inflect. Both FII and DII buying. Trailing 90x PE looks absurd but forward earnings on a recovering base tell a different story.
stocks.risks: 90x trailing PE only works if the turnaround holds; historically poor ROCE (9%); execution risk.

stocks.name: NCC Ltd
stocks.thesis: The cheapest construction play — 12.9x PE, 1.15x book, 1.52% dividend, near its 52-week low — leveraged to the ₹12.2 lakh cr FY27 capex budget and Jal Jeevan. Deep value in a high-policy theme; the market simply hasn't noticed yet.
stocks.risks: Latest quarter -8%; low promoter holding (22.8%); contractor receivables/leverage; NHAI awarding has been muted.

stocks.name: MTAR Technologies
stocks.thesis: SHORT / AVOID on price, not theme. A real nuclear/defence precision-engineering beneficiary — but 214x PE against a 12.5% ROE and 16% CAGR is the most extreme valuation in the entire universe. The theme is a decade long; the price already assumes it all went perfectly.
stocks.risks: 214x PE; latest quarter profit down; any de-rating toward even 45x implies ~70% downside.

stocks.name: Rail Vikas Nigam
stocks.thesis: TACTICAL SHORT. 55x PE on a railway EPC with NEGATIVE 5-yr profit CAGR, a -32% latest quarter, and screener-flagged working-capital deterioration (debtor days 47→96). Earnings padded by ₹869 cr other income; promoter (govt) trimming. Flat FY27 railway capex removes the catalyst.
stocks.risks: Over-owned policy-froth theme rolling over; a squeeze is possible in a broad rally.

stocks.name: Bharat Dynamics
stocks.thesis: SHORT / AVOID on price. Missile-maker with real strategic importance — but the weakest quality in defence (10% ROE, 10% 5-yr CAGR) at the highest multiple (114x PE). Priced on order-book hope, not delivery.
stocks.risks: 114x PE on 10% CAGR; FII exiting; a genuine theme wrapped around an un-investable valuation.

stocks.name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders
stocks.thesis: TACTICAL SHORT (Kotak: Sell, TP ₹1,950). Warship-builder whose latest quarter profit fell ~20% YoY on revenue -18% — a real earnings decline, not optics. Down ~27% from its high, below trend. Kotak models margins normalizing 17.4%→14.6%. Defence-yard froth deflating on actual numbers.
stocks.risks: Excellent ROCE (36%) and order book make it a business worth owning eventually — just not at 48x with margins normalizing.

stocks.name: Titagarh Rail Systems
stocks.thesis: SHORT / AVOID. Rolling-stock maker with a big order book (>₹28,000 cr incl. the ₹24,000 cr BHEL trainset deal) — but 72x PE against a 6.5% ROE and a shrinking latest quarter, with FIIs steadily exiting. Flat FY27 railway capex removes the growth surprise.
stocks.risks: 72x PE on 6.5% ROE; profit shrinking; a squeeze risk if railways re-rate as a group.

stocks.name: Cochin Shipyard

holders.trend: FII out over multi-year; promoter cut ~5% over 3yr

stocks.thesis: AVOID / UNDERPERFORM. 52x PE against just 4% 5-yr CAGR, a -13% latest quarter, the weakest returns and highest debt in the shipbuilding trio, plus promoter selling. Same defence-yard de-rating vector as Mazagon with less order visibility.
stocks.risks: Poor fundamentals-to-multiple; ₹211bn order book is the offsetting bull case.

stocks.name: Trent Ltd
stocks.thesis: TACTICAL SHORT / UNDERPERFORM (best retail short). A genuinely great business (Zudio/Westside, ROCE 28%) — but growth decelerated to +8.7% from the 40-50% that earned its 90x multiple, it is below its 200-DMA, ~23% off its high, and FIIs have halved their stake. Momentum + deceleration + rich multiple is the classic underperform setup. NOT a budget-priority sector.
stocks.risks: Quality is real, so size as underperform not a blow-up; consumption could re-accelerate.

stocks.name: FSN E-Commerce (Nykaa)
stocks.thesis: AVOID (hard to short outright). 476x PE and 66x book on a structurally thin ~8% ROE — a caricature of a rich multiple, with Sell ratings from Macquarie/Citi/HDFC. The danger: the +316% latest-quarter profit is inflecting off a tiny base, so a naked short can get squeezed. Rich-multiple avoid, not a clean tactical short.
stocks.risks: Earnings inflecting off a small base makes shorting dangerous; borrow/squeeze risk.

stocks.name: Asian Paints
stocks.thesis: AVOID / UNDERPERFORM. Elite franchise (ROCE 26%) but a structural stall — ~1% 3-yr sales growth, ~2% profit — as Birla Opus takes share. 59x PE for near-zero growth. CLSA Underperform. A de-rating story, not an earnings collapse.
stocks.risks: Fortress balance sheet and brand mean it won't crater — just likely lag; input-cost/competition swing factors.
